JavaScript Core Concepts (32) - Objects - JSON: It Looks Like an Object, But It Isn't

JSON looks almost exactly like a JavaScript object, which is exactly why people keep tripping over it. In this post we'll look at what JSON actually is, the rules that make it different from a JS object (double quotes only, no trailing comma), and how to convert back and forth with JSON.stringify() and JSON.parse().
JavaScript Objects: Call by Reference or Call by Sharing? (Core Concepts 29)

Is JavaScript Call by Reference or Call by Sharing? In this post we walk through evaluation strategies — Call by value, Call by reference, and Call by sharing — with simple examples, and figure out which one JavaScript actually uses when you pass objects around.
JavaScript Core Concepts (23) — Objects: Variables vs Object Properties

A quick note on the difference between variables and object properties in JavaScript. Declaring a variable adds a property on window — but so does assigning to an undeclared name like b = 2. The key difference is that a proper variable can't be deleted, while an accidental global one can. Here's why you should always declare your variables.
